text:
Ivlenkov (イワレンコフ, Iwarenkofu) was one of Neon Nostrade's bodyguards.

Appearance

Ivlenkov was a plump man with brown combed-back hair and green eyes. He was only seen wearing a standard black suit, a white shirt, and a black tie. In the 2011 anime adaptation, his tie was dark red.

Personality

Ivlenkov always had a calm and professional demeanor, especially around his client Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 71 and while attending the Underground Auction as one of the representatives for the Nostrade Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 73 

Plot

Yorknew City arc

On a private runway at Lingon Airport, Ivlenkov, Linssen, Tocino, Dalzollene, and Squala keep a close eye on Neon Nostrade as she exits her private airship and makes her way by car toward Yorknew City. Ivlenkov, Tocino, and Baise are assigned by Dalzollene to attend the Underground Auction as representatives for the Nostrade Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 72 

Before the start of the auction, the three note all the different capos in attendance. Once Franklin begins to use his Double Machine Gun ability the massacre the Mafia members, Ivlenkov breaks down the door so that he and Baise can escape, but he is immediately killed by Shizuku, who uses Blinky to crush his skull. His corpse is then inhaled by Shizuku's vacuum cleaner. 

Kurapika mentions Ivlenkov to Uvogin as one of the Nostrade bodyguards who was killed during the auction massacre. After Uvogin shows little remorse for their deaths, Kurapika angrily punches him in the face. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 78 Neon is eventually told about the deaths of Ivlenkov, Baise, and Tocino, but she is only concerned about buying the mummy of Princess Corco. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 79 

While checking the Hunter Website, Kurapika sees pictures and descriptions of Neon, Ivlenkov, and the other bodyguards (besides himself and Melody). Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 96 

Abilities & Powers

Ivlenkov used to be a Nostrade bodyguard and, as such, must have been skilled enough to have passed a test for the position.

Advanced Speed: He proved to be moderately fast and agile when he dodged the hail of Nen bullets fired by Franklin's Double Machine Gun. 

Nen

His Nen type and abilities are unknown since they were never shown or mentioned.

Manga Appearances

Battles

Trivia

In the English dub of the 1999 anime adaptation, he was referred to as "Ilankov".

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, Ivlenkov throws a Nen-imbued chair at Franklin during the Underground Auction, which Franklin destroys with his Nen bullets. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 52 (1999)
